chunks.text: The human body is an astonishingly complex system made up of about thirty - seven trillion cell.
The heart beats about 100,000 times a day
and the lungs breathe in and out about 20,000 times a day.
The food we eat is digested in the stomach and intestines
and is delivered as energy to the tissue across the body.
Nutrients such as protein, vitamins, and minerals are essential for the body's repair and growth.
The three pillars of staying healthy are said to be moderate exercise, a balanced diet
and sufficient sleep.
When these are broken, the risk of various disease rises.
Lack of exercise can cause heart disease and diabetes.
Too much salt or sugar leads to high blood pressure and obesity.
Lack of sleep weakens the immune system and makes a person more likely to fall ill.
Stress affects not only mental health, but also physical health, in serious ways.
Chronic stress can cause digestive trouble, headaches, sleeplessness, and depression.
Regular medical checks help to detect disease early.
Physician listen to a patient's lifestyle and, when needed, suggest a treatment.
The body is the most precious tool that has been given to each of us.
The small choices of each day support a long life.
Health is not something we attend to only on special occasions
but something that lives in the daily fabric of our lives.
